step_by_step_thinking:
The ulcer likely resulted from leakage of formocresol onto the lip during or after the procedure as this chemical can be highly irritating and corrosive if it comes into contact with tissues outside its intended application area. Given that the lesion was located near the midline where the rubber dam might not have been perfectly positioned over the tooth being treated, option D seems most plausible. Answer: D.
You are correct in identifying that the ulcer is likely due to leakage of formocresol (dental cementic acid). Formocresol can indeed cause severe tissue damage upon accidental exposure beyond the site of treatment. The location near the midline suggests potential inadequate placement of the rubber dam, making Option D the most probable cause among those provided.
